name: Isolde
description: Isolde, also known as Iseult or Isolda, is a central figure in the Arthurian romance of Tristan and Isolde. She is often depicted as a beautiful Irish princess, daughter of King Anguish of Ireland and Queen Isolde. Her story is one of tragic love and destiny, marked by a powerful love potion that binds her to Tristan, a knight of King Mark of Cornwall. Isolde is initially betrothed to King Mark, creating a complex love triangle that drives much of the narrative. She is known for her beauty, grace, and healing abilities, as well as her unwavering loyalty to Tristan, even at the cost of her own reputation and happiness. The tale explores themes of forbidden love, courtly love, duty, and the conflict between personal desires and societal expectations. The tragic consequences of their affair, leading to their eventual deaths, solidify Isolde's place as a symbol of passionate, yet ultimately doomed, romance in Arthurian legend. Her character is often portrayed as intelligent and resourceful, navigating the treacherous waters of courtly intrigue while battling her own conflicting emotions. Isolde's enduring appeal lies in her complex portrayal as both a victim of fate and an active participant in her own destiny, making her a compelling and unforgettable figure in the Arthurian canon. The different versions of the story highlight different aspects of her character, sometimes emphasizing her agency and other times portraying her as a more passive figure swept up by events beyond her control. Ultimately, Isolde represents the powerful and destructive force of love, a force that transcends social boundaries and leads to both ecstasy and despair.
subtitle: A tragic tale of forbidden love.
achievements: Becoming a symbol of tragic love

keyMyths.title: The Love Potion
keyMyths.description: Isolde and Tristan accidentally drink a love potion prepared for Isolde and King Mark. This potion binds them together in an inescapable, passionate love.
keyMyths.source: Various versions of Tristan and Isolde

keyMyths.title: The Trial by Ordeal
keyMyths.description: Isolde is forced to undergo a trial to prove her fidelity to King Mark. She cleverly deceives the court, maintaining her secret affair with Tristan.
keyMyths.source: Various versions of Tristan and Isolde

keyMyths.title: The Death of Tristan and Isolde
keyMyths.description: Tristan, mortally wounded, sends for Isolde, whose arrival will signal his survival. A jealous Isolde of Brittany lies, leading to Tristan's death, followed by Isolde's of grief.
keyMyths.source: Various versions of Tristan and Isolde

sources.author: Thomas of Britain
sources.description: An influential version of the Tristan and Isolde story, though incomplete.
sources.text: An influential version of the Tristan and Isolde story, though incomplete.
